channel.title: morningNewsSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Xi and Kim express hopes for stronger China-North Korea ties
item.description: Xi visited Pyongyang on Monday, likely aiming to reaffirm China's unique influence over its socialist neighbor.

item.title: NPR's new chief content officer: 'I've been training for this my whole life'
item.description: Two weeks after restructuring its newsroom, NPR appointed Nadine Zylstra as chief content officer. She previously held a top role at Sesame Workshop.

item.title: With days remaining, the U.S. World Cup opener has not yet sold out
item.description: Hundreds of tickets remain for the U.S. and Canada opening World Cup matches on Friday, with many more on resale platforms.

item.title: Israel-Iran strikes threaten ceasefire. Ebola spreads at record pace
item.description: Recent exchanges of fire between Israel and Iran are endangering the Middle East truce. The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is...
